description: Dietary polyphenol supplements evaluated for ABCG2 (BCRP) inhibition at gut-lumen concentrations

compounds.name: Quercetin
compounds.solubility_note: Aqueous ~7 mg/L (pH 7); intestinal estimate ~15 mg/L accounting for bile salts and pH 6.5-7.0. Solubility is dose-limiting at supplement doses.
compounds.bioavailability_source: Manach 2005, PMID 15640486 — mean bioavailability ~17% across studies

compounds.name: EGCG
compounds.solubility_note: EGCG is water-soluble; ~500 mg/L is conservative. Dose-limiting factor is bioavailability, not solubility.
compounds.bioavailability_source: Literature consensus ~2%; absorbed mainly as glucuronide/sulfate conjugates
compounds.mechanism_note: EGCG modulates ABCG2 via expression downregulation (mRNA/protein level, 24-72h), not acute transport inhibition. The IC50 occupancy framework does not apply. See ic50_data.json for details.

compounds.name: Curcumin
compounds.solubility_note: Aqueous ~0.6 mg/L (virtually insoluble). In intestinal fluid with bile/lipids: ~2-8 mg/L; 5 mg/L used as central estimate. Solubility is the dominant dose-limiting constraint.
compounds.bioavailability_source: Metzler 2012, PMID 22996406 — <1% systemic bioavailability; essentially all oral dose remains in gut lumen
